about.heading: About us

paragraphs.intro: Aero Metric is a Szczecin-based company specialising in professional drone services for business and institutional clients. We combine advanced technology with hands-on experience to deliver precise aerial data and analysis.
paragraphs.industries: We serve clients in the industrial, agricultural, energy, and infrastructure sectors. Our services include drone inspections, photogrammetry, thermal imaging, 3D modelling, and wildlife damage estimation. We operate across Poland and Germany.
paragraphs.commitment: We prioritise safety, precision, and timely delivery. Every project is carried out with meticulous care, using modern equipment and proven methods.

meta.title: Drone photogrammetry Szczecin | 3D models & orthophotomaps
meta.description: Drone photogrammetry in Szczecin. Orthophotomaps, point clouds and 3D models with 1-3 cm accuracy.

hero.heading: Drone photogrammetry in Szczecin and across Poland
hero.subheading: We create precise orthophotomaps, point clouds and 3D models from drone-acquired data. Our photogrammetric products serve the surveying, construction and agriculture sectors across Poland.

intro: Drone photogrammetry produces precise 3D models, orthophotomaps and point clouds of terrain or built structures. We acquire data using a drone equipped with an RGB camera of up to 42 Mpx and a GNSS RTK receiver, achieving horizontal and vertical accuracy of 1-3 cm without the need for ground control points.

This service is aimed at surveyors, construction firms, property managers, local authorities and development investors. We carry out building inventories, stockpile and excavation volume measurements, construction site documentation, agricultural land orthophotomaps and 3D models of heritage structures.

Compared with traditional total-station surveying or terrestrial laser scanning, drone photogrammetry is 3-5 times faster and covers a larger area in a single survey session. A flight over 10 ha of terrain takes approximately 20-30 minutes, and we deliver the complete processed dataset within 3-5 working days.

benefits.title: 1-3 cm accuracy without ground control points
benefits.description: A drone with a GNSS RTK receiver achieves centimetre-level georeferencing accuracy without the need to place ground-based GCP markers.

benefits.title: 3-5 times faster than ground-based surveying
benefits.description: A flight over 10 ha of terrain takes approx. 25 minutes. A traditional total-station survey of the same area would require 2-3 working days.

benefits.title: Ready-made formats for GIS, CAD and BIM
benefits.description: We deliver orthophotomaps in GeoTIFF, point clouds in LAS/LAZ, mesh models in OBJ/PLY -- ready for direct import into your software.

benefits.title: Complete terrain documentation from a single flight
benefits.description: From one flight we generate an orthophotomap, DTM, DSM, point cloud and 3D model -- a full dataset for multiple types of analysis.

useCases.title: Building inventory for property managers
useCases.description: A 3D model of a residential or office building with 2 cm accuracy -- the basis for planning renovations, extensions and refurbishments without multiple site visits.

useCases.title: Stockpile and excavation volume measurements
useCases.description: Calculating volumes of stored earthworks, gravel or aggregate on construction sites and open-pit mines -- volumetric report delivered within 48 hours.

useCases.title: Construction progress monitoring every 2-4 weeks
useCases.description: Regular aerial surveys documenting successive stages of a project. Comparing orthophotomaps and 3D models against the design helps detect delays and deviations.

useCases.title: Field maps for precision agriculture
useCases.description: Crop field maps at 2 cm/px resolution supporting fertilisation, spraying and irrigation planning. Data exported in formats compatible with agricultural machinery software.

process.title: Contact and requirements analysis
process.description: We define the project scope: terrain type, required accuracy, output data formats and delivery deadline.

process.title: Flight mission planning
process.description: We set the flight parameters -- altitude, image overlap, ground sampling distance (GSD). We obtain permits if the site requires CAA clearance.

process.title: Drone flight and image acquisition
process.description: We fly an automated mission along a pre-programmed route. For a 10 ha site, the flight takes approx. 25 minutes.

process.title: Photogrammetric data processing
process.description: Aerial images are processed in photogrammetric software, generating the orthophotomap, point cloud, DTM and 3D model.

process.title: Delivery of materials
process.description: We deliver the finished files in the agreed formats via a download link or data carrier. Typical turnaround: 3-5 working days from the flight.

deliverables.heading: What the client receives
items: Orthophotomap in GeoTIFF format (1-3 cm/px resolution)
Point cloud in LAS/LAZ format
3D mesh model in OBJ or PLY format
Digital Terrain Model (DTM) and Digital Surface Model (DSM)
Accuracy report (GSD, RMSE, flight parameters)

area.heading: Area of operation
area.text: Based in Szczecin. We carry out commissions across the West Pomeranian Voivodeship and -- by prior arrangement -- throughout Poland. We travel to the site in our own vehicle; travel up to 50 km from Szczecin is included in the price.

cta.heading: Need an orthophotomap or 3D model?
cta.text: Get in touch and we will prepare a free drone photogrammetry quote tailored to your project.

meta.title: Thermal imaging drone Szczecin | Roofs, halls & solar panels
meta.description: Drone thermal imaging in Szczecin. Detecting heat loss, solar panel faults and insulation issues in roofs and halls.

hero.heading: Thermal imaging by drone — roofs, industrial halls & PV systems
hero.subheading: We use a drone-mounted thermal camera to detect heat loss, diagnose solar panel faults and inspect roof insulation. Fast, safe and with centimetre precision.

intro: We carry out thermal inspections of flat and pitched roofs, building facades, industrial halls and photovoltaic farms using a drone fitted with a radiometric thermal camera (640 x 512 px resolution). The camera records the temperature of every pixel, enabling precise localisation of thermal anomalies -- thermal bridges, damp, air leaks and faulty PV cells.

This service is aimed at property managers, construction firms, energy auditors, warehouse owners and PV farm operators. Drone thermography replaces ground-level inspection (which cannot cover roof surfaces) and cherry-picker inspection (which is slower and more expensive).

Every inspection concludes with a PDF report containing thermograms with a temperature scale overlay, corresponding RGB photographs, a description and location of each anomaly, and recommendations for remedial action. We deliver the report within 2-3 working days of the flight.

benefits.title: Full roof coverage without scaffolding or cherry pickers
benefits.description: The drone covers the entire roof surface in a single flight -- even halls exceeding 5,000 m2. No need to close the building or arrange roof access.

benefits.title: Detection of defects invisible to the naked eye
benefits.description: The thermal camera identifies thermal bridges, damp beneath the waterproofing layer, air leaks around flashings and faulty PV cells with 5 cm precision.

benefits.title: Report with thermograms and recommendations
benefits.description: The PDF report contains thermograms, RGB images, anomaly locations on a roof plan, cause descriptions and specific repair recommendations -- ready to hand to a contractor.

benefits.title: PV farm inspection: 1 MW in 30 minutes
benefits.description: Drone thermography can scan a 1 MW PV farm in approx. 30 minutes. The report identifies faulty strings, hotspots and mechanical panel damage.

useCases.title: Thermal inspection of a warehouse roof
useCases.description: Detecting heat loss and damp on flat roofs of halls ranging from several hundred to several thousand m2 -- the basis for planning roof covering repairs.

useCases.title: Photovoltaic farm diagnostics
useCases.description: Scanning PV panels for faulty cells, hotspots, mechanical damage and shading. A defect map report facilitates service planning.

useCases.title: Building energy audit support
useCases.description: Non-contact assessment of facade insulation, window frames and roof-wall junctions -- supplementary data for the energy audit required for thermal modernisation.

useCases.title: Thermal monitoring of pipelines and tanks
useCases.description: Early detection of hot water, steam or chemical media leaks in industrial installations -- without interrupting plant operations.

process.title: Contact and scope definition
process.description: We determine the object type (roof, facade, PV farm, industrial installation), its area and the inspection objective.

process.title: Selecting conditions and scheduling
process.description: Thermal inspection requires suitable conditions -- a minimum 10 C temperature difference between interior and exterior, no rain and low wind. We propose the optimal date.

process.title: Drone flight with thermal camera
process.description: We fly over the object recording thermograms and RGB images with overlay. A typical warehouse roof takes approx. 15-30 minutes.

process.title: Analysis and report preparation
process.description: We analyse the thermograms, identify anomalies, describe causes and prepare a PDF report with repair recommendations.

deliverables.heading: What the client receives
items: PDF report with thermograms, RGB images and anomaly descriptions
Anomaly map overlaid on the building plan
Prioritised repair recommendations
Raw thermogram files (RJPG) on request
Thermal orthophotomap of the object (optional)

area.heading: Area of operation
area.text: We carry out thermal inspections from our base in Szczecin across the West Pomeranian and Lubusz Voivodeships. By prior arrangement, we accept commissions throughout Poland. Travel up to 50 km from Szczecin is included in the price.

cta.heading: Need a thermal inspection?
cta.text: Get in touch and we will recommend the optimal scope of thermal survey for your property.

meta.title: 3D models from drone Szczecin | Digital terrain & building models
meta.description: 3D models from drone data in Szczecin. Digital terrain models, building inventory and mesh models for construction.

hero.heading: 3D models and digital terrain maps from drone data
hero.subheading: We create digital terrain models, realistic 3D mesh models and building inventories from photogrammetric drone data. Accuracy down to 2 cm -- ready for import into CAD and BIM software.

intro: We create digital 3D models of buildings, industrial structures and terrain from aerial photographs acquired by drone. From hundreds of overlapping images we generate a dense point cloud and then a 3D mesh model with photographic texture -- faithfully reproducing the appearance and geometry of the object.

This service is aimed at construction firms, design offices, surveyors, local authorities and property managers. Drone 3D models are used for building inventory before refurbishment, creating terrain maps for development sites, heritage documentation and construction progress visualisation -- without the need for repeated laser scanning.

Drone inventory is 3-5 times faster than terrestrial laser scanning and covers a larger area in a single session. A 3D model of a building with a 500 m2 footprint is delivered within 5 working days of the flight in OBJ, PLY or FBX format -- ready for import into Autodesk, ArchiCAD or SketchUp.

benefits.title: Centimetre-level geometric accuracy
benefits.description: 3D models reproduce the shape and dimensions of an object with 1-3 cm accuracy -- sufficient for designing refurbishments, extensions and clash detection.

benefits.title: Digital Terrain Model (DTM) and Surface Model (DSM)
benefits.description: We generate elevation models in GeoTIFF and ASC formats -- ready for hydrological analysis, road design and investment planning.

benefits.title: Faster and cheaper than laser scanning
benefits.description: Drone inventory of a 500 m2 building takes 1 working day instead of the 3-4 days required for terrestrial laser scanning -- with comparable accuracy.

benefits.title: Export to CAD, BIM and GIS
benefits.description: We deliver models in OBJ, PLY, FBX, DXF and GeoTIFF formats -- compatible with Autodesk Revit, ArchiCAD, Civil 3D, QGIS and ArcGIS.

useCases.title: 3D building inventory before refurbishment
useCases.description: A 3D model of a townhouse, office building or industrial hall -- the basis for designing a refurbishment without multiple measurement visits to the site.

useCases.title: Digital terrain model for a development site
useCases.description: DTM and DSM of a plot or terrain up to several dozen hectares -- for road design, flood risk analysis and spatial planning.

useCases.title: 3D construction progress visualisation
useCases.description: Comparing 3D models from successive months against the design model (BIM) -- for detecting delays and deviations from the project.

useCases.title: 3D documentation of heritage and cultural sites
useCases.description: Precise 3D models of churches, townhouses and heritage structures -- for conservators, architects and reconstruction designers.

process.title: Contact and project specification
process.description: We define the scope: object type, required accuracy, output file formats and delivery deadline.

process.title: Drone flight and image acquisition
process.description: We fly an automated mission around the object or over the terrain, capturing several hundred images with min. 75% overlap. Building -- approx. 20 min, 10 ha terrain -- approx. 30 min.

process.title: Data processing and model generation
process.description: Images are processed in photogrammetric software. We generate the point cloud, textured mesh model and digital terrain models.

process.title: Quality control and delivery
process.description: We verify model accuracy, export data in the agreed formats and deliver to the client via download link. Typical turnaround: 5-7 working days.

deliverables.heading: What the client receives
items: 3D mesh model with photographic texture in OBJ, PLY or FBX format
Dense point cloud in LAS/LAZ format
Digital Terrain Model (DTM) in GeoTIFF or ASC format
Digital Surface Model (DSM)
Accuracy and processing parameters report

area.heading: Area of operation
area.text: We carry out 3D inventories from our base in Szczecin across the West Pomeranian Voivodeship. Commissions in other regions of Poland are accepted by prior arrangement. Travel up to 50 km from Szczecin is included in the price.

cta.heading: Need a 3D model or digital terrain model?
cta.text: Get in touch and we will prepare a quote for a 3D model tailored to the scale and requirements of your project.

meta.title: Wildlife damage assessment by drone | Expert reports & PDF
meta.description: Wildlife damage estimation by drone in Poland. Crop damage assessment, expert PDF reports and compliant documentation.

hero.heading: Wildlife damage estimation by drone — expert reports & PDF
hero.subheading: We carry out wildlife damage assessments using drones across the West Pomeranian Voivodeship, throughout Poland and in Germany. Rapid response, precise area measurements and documentation compliant with Ministry of Environment regulations.

intro: We fly drones over crop fields where damage caused by wild boar, deer, roe deer and other game has occurred. From the aerial photographs we generate an orthophotomap of the field, on which we precisely delineate the boundaries of the damaged crop and calculate its area in square metres and the percentage of destruction -- data that forms the basis of the official expert report.

This service is aimed at farmers, hunting associations, expert assessors and insurers. A drone orthophotomap eliminates the subjectivity of manual measurement -- the damaged area is calculated with geodetic accuracy (1-3 cm), and the documentation is resistant to challenge in compensation proceedings.

Time is the critical factor: the condition of a damaged crop changes from day to day, so documentation must be produced as quickly as possible after notification. Thanks to drone mobility, we can reach the field and complete the survey within hours of contact -- even on the same day.

benefits.title: Geodetically precise area measurement
benefits.description: A drone orthophotomap calculates the damaged area with 1-3 cm accuracy -- eliminating disputes about the scale of losses between the farmer and the hunting association.

benefits.title: Same-day survey after notification
benefits.description: In urgent cases we travel to the field on the same day. Speed of response is critical because crop condition changes from day to day.

benefits.title: Documentation compliant with regulations
benefits.description: The expert report contains an orthophotomap, area calculations, damage percentage and photographic documentation -- in accordance with the Ministry of Environment regulations.

benefits.title: Evidence for legal proceedings
benefits.description: The drone orthophotomap and PDF expert report constitute credible evidence in compensation disputes between farmers and hunting associations.

useCases.title: Wild boar damage to maize and cereals
useCases.description: Documenting destruction in maize, wheat and rapeseed fields caused by wild boar -- the most common type of hunting damage in the West Pomeranian region.

useCases.title: Deer and roe deer damage to winter crops
useCases.description: Estimating losses to winter crops and young forestry plantations caused by deer, roe deer and fallow deer grazing -- orthophotomap and PDF expert report.

useCases.title: Objective data for the hunting association assessment committee
useCases.description: Providing an orthophotomap and area calculations for the assessment committee -- eliminating disputes over subjective visual estimates.

useCases.title: Photogrammetric documentation for court proceedings
useCases.description: Preparing professional documentation with orthophotomap, calculations and GPS metadata -- serving as evidence in legal proceedings.

process.title: Damage notification
process.description: The farmer or hunting association contacts us by phone or email. We establish the field location, crop type and approximate extent of damage.

process.title: Drone flight over the field
process.description: We travel to the field and fly the drone. A flight over a 5-10 ha field takes approx. 15-25 minutes.

process.title: Data processing and area calculation
process.description: We generate an orthophotomap of the field, delineate the boundaries of the damaged crop and calculate the damaged area in m2 and the destruction percentage.

process.title: Expert report preparation (PDF)
process.description: The report contains the orthophotomap, calculations, photographic documentation and flight metadata. We deliver it within 2-3 working days of the flight.

deliverables.heading: What the client receives
items: Expert report in PDF format (compliant with regulations)
Orthophotomap of the field with damage boundaries marked
Damaged area calculation (m2) and destruction percentage
Flight photographic documentation (full-resolution RGB images)
Flight metadata: date, GPS coordinates, drone parameters

area.heading: Area of operation
area.text: We carry out wildlife damage assessments across the West Pomeranian Voivodeship, and by prior arrangement in other regions of Poland and in Germany (Mecklenburg-Vorpommern, Brandenburg). In urgent cases we can reach the field on the same day.

cta.heading: Wildlife damage? We respond fast.
cta.text: Contact us by phone or email — in urgent cases, we can conduct a survey on the same day.

meta.title: Wind turbine inspection by drone | Blades & nacelle surveys
meta.description: Drone inspection of wind turbines in Szczecin. Blade, nacelle and tower surveys without halting turbine operation.

hero.heading: Drone inspection of wind turbines — blades, nacelles & masts
hero.subheading: We carry out visual inspections of wind turbine blades, nacelles and towers using drones. Rapid identification of cracks, erosion and lightning damage -- without rope-access technicians and without halting energy production.

intro: We carry out visual inspections of wind turbines using a drone equipped with a 42 Mpx camera and optical zoom. The drone approaches the rotor blades, nacelle and tower at a distance of 3-5 metres, capturing images at a resolution that allows identification of cracks, delamination, leading-edge erosion and lightning damage.

This service is aimed at wind farm operators, turbine service providers and insurance companies. Drone inspection is 3-5 times faster than inspection using rope-access technicians and does not require a turbine shutdown -- the blades only need to be locked in the inspection position.

Every inspection concludes with a photographic report in which each identified defect is located on a blade/nacelle/tower diagram, described and classified according to a severity scale (1 -- monitor, 2 -- repair at next service, 3 -- urgent repair). The report enables advance planning of inspections and repairs.

benefits.title: Inspection without turbine shutdown
benefits.description: The blades only need to be locked in the inspection position. The turbine does not need to be disconnected from the grid -- minimising energy production losses.

benefits.title: 3-5 times faster than rope-access methods
benefits.description: A drone inspection of one turbine takes 1-2 hours. Rope-access technicians need 6-8 hours plus equipment setup time for the same scope.

benefits.title: Defect imagery from 3-5 metres distance
benefits.description: The drone camera captures cracks narrower than 1 mm, leading-edge erosion, lightning strike marks and laminate delamination at close range.

benefits.title: Report with defect severity classification
benefits.description: Each defect is located on a turbine diagram, described and classified: 1 -- monitor, 2 -- repair at service, 3 -- urgent repair.

useCases.title: Rotor blade inspection after the windy season
useCases.description: Inspection of all three blades for cracks, delamination, leading-edge erosion and lightning damage -- defect classification report within 3 days.

useCases.title: Nacelle and housing inspection
useCases.description: Assessment of the nacelle housing condition, lightning protection system, seals and externally visible mechanical components.

useCases.title: Periodic inspection of an entire wind farm
useCases.description: Planned inspection of 10-20 turbines over 3-5 days -- a consolidated report for the farm operator with repair prioritisation.

useCases.title: Post-incident inspection after lightning strike
useCases.description: Rapid drone inspection within 24-48 hours of the event. Damage documentation for the manufacturer, insurer and repair planning.

process.title: Scope and schedule agreement
process.description: We establish the number of turbines, inspection scope (blades, nacelle, tower) and schedule. We coordinate site access with the operator.

process.title: Turbine preparation
process.description: The operator locks the blades in the inspection position (each blade is rotated to the 6 o'clock position). The turbine does not need to be disconnected from the grid.

process.title: Drone inspection
process.description: The pilot flies around each blade, nacelle and tower, capturing images from 3-5 m distance. One turbine takes approx. 1-2 hours.

process.title: Image analysis and report
process.description: We review the images, identify defects, classify them and prepare a photographic report with a defect map. Typical turnaround: 3-5 working days.

deliverables.heading: What the client receives
items: Photographic PDF report with location, description and classification of each defect
Blade/nacelle/tower diagram with defects marked
High-resolution images of every identified defect
Summary with repair prioritisation (monitor / repair / urgent)
Raw flight images at full resolution on a data carrier

area.heading: Area of operation
area.text: We carry out wind turbine inspections across the West Pomeranian, Pomeranian and Greater Poland Voivodeships -- regions with the highest concentration of wind farms in Poland. By arrangement we accept commissions nationwide.

cta.heading: Planning a wind turbine inspection?
cta.text: Get in touch and we will prepare a drone inspection quote tailored to the size of your wind farm and scope of survey.
